title: DNA Repair (NHEJ vs HDR)
summary: After Cas9 creates a double-strand break, cellular repair pathways determine whether the outcome is an indel, precise sequence change, or donor-template-mediated insertion.

sections.heading: NHEJ
sections.content: Non-homologous end joining ligates broken DNA ends without requiring a homologous donor template. It is active in many cell states and often introduces small insertions or deletions that can disrupt coding sequence or regulatory elements.

sections.heading: HDR
sections.content: Homology-directed repair uses a donor template with homology arms to introduce defined edits. HDR is generally most active in S/G2 phases and is often less efficient than NHEJ in many experimental systems.

key_points: NHEJ is commonly used for gene disruption.
HDR enables precise edits but usually requires a donor template.
Repair pathway bias depends on cell type, cell cycle, delivery format, and locus context.
limitations: Repair outcomes are heterogeneous. Precise editing strategies require careful donor design, genotyping, and assessment of unintended indels or rearrangements.
